article_title: Slay the Spire 2 Sells 3.3 Million Copies in Its First Week of Early Access, Shattering Steam Records for the Roguelike Genre
summary: Factual discrepancy: article claims 3.3 million copies but both cited sources say 3 million

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Slay the Spire 2 Sells 3.3 Million Copies in Its First Week of Early Access, Shattering Steam Records for the Roguelike Genre
content_preview.summary: Mega Crit's sequel hit 574,000 peak concurrent players on Steam and generated an estimated $75 million in its opening week, nearly matching the original's entire lifetime sales.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Slay the Spire 2, the sequel to Mega Crit Games' 2019 roguelike deckbuilder, sold 3.3 million copies within its first week of Early Access on Steam, the developer [announced on March 14](https://www.pcgamer.com/games/card-games/even-though-i-threw-out-my-back-from-overworking-im-feeling-high-in-spirits-slay-the-spire-2-sold-3-million-copies-in-its-first-week/). The game launched on March 5 at a price of $25 and reached a peak of 574,638 concurrent players, [making it the largest rog...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured gaming news at 542 words with good Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis sections. Writing quality is strong.
editor_notes.source_verification: PC Gamer — ✅ Confirmed: first week sales, 574,638 concurrent, $25 price, March 5 launch, original 3.6M lifetime, 94% rating, five-year dev cycle, two-person studio, Demi quote, planned features. BUT: PC Gamer says '3 million copies' not '3.3 million.' The URL itself contains 'sold-3-million-copies.' Kotaku — ✅ Confirmed: ~$75M revenue estimate, HP exploit patch (999,999,999 cap), Early Access status. BUT: also says '3 million copies sold,' not 3.3 million. Also says '250 million+ total runs' and '80 runs per person' — article says '25 million runs' (may be a misread of one source). GameSpot — returned 403, could not verify 'largest roguelike launch in Steam history' claim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: CRITICAL ISSUE: Article states '3.3 million copies' in title, summary, and body. Both PC Gamer and Kotaku consistently report '3 million copies.' The 0.3 million discrepancy is unsupported by the cited sources and inflates the headline figure. SECONDARY ISSUE: Article says '25 million runs' — Kotaku reports '250 million+ total runs' and '80 runs per person.' At 3M copies × 80 runs = 240M, the 250M figure is mathematically consistent. The 25M figure may be a misread or from an earlier data point. The $75M revenue calculation also changes: 3M × $25 = $75M still works, but 3.3M × $25 = $82.5M (the article says $75M while claiming 3.3M copies, which is internally inconsistent).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism beyond the inflated sales figure.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Slay the Spire articles in the archive. Topic is new.
concerns: Article claims 3.3 million copies sold but both PC Gamer and Kotaku report 3 million — this is a factual error that appears in the title, summary, and body
The $75M revenue estimate is calculated at 3M × $25, but the article simultaneously claims 3.3M copies, creating an internal inconsistency
25 million runs vs Kotaku's 250 million runs — significant discrepancy that should be investigated
recommendations: Correct '3.3 million' to '3 million' (or 'more than 3 million') throughout the article — title, summary, and body
Verify the runs figure: PC Gamer may say 25 million but Kotaku reports 250 million+. Use the most recent/accurate figure
Adjust the revenue estimate to be consistent with the corrected sales figure
GameSpot source returned 403 — consider finding an alternative source for the 'largest roguelike launch' claim, or attribute it differently
editor_notes.overall_assessment: The article is well-written but contains a factual error in its headline figure. The cited sources say 3 million copies, not 3.3 million. This must be corrected before publication as it inflates the core news figure.
